description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: South Carolina (  KARR-ə-LY-nə) is a state in the Southeastern, South Atlantic and Deep South regions of the United States.

description: town in New York
description_wiki: Esopus ( eh-SOHP-əs) is a town in Ulster County, New York, United States.

description: Reformed Christian denomination in the Netherlands
description_wiki: The Dutch Reformed Church (Dutch: Nederlandse Hervormde Kerk, pronounced [ˈneːdərlɑntsə ɦɛrˈvɔr(ə)mdə ˈkɛr(ə)k], abbreviated NHK [ˌɛnɦaːˈkaː]) was the largest Christian denomination in the Netherlands from the onset of the Protestant Reformation in the 16th century until 1930.

description: American minister (1755-1825)
description_wiki: Richard Furman (October 9, 1755 – August 25, 1825) was an American Baptist leader from Charleston, South Carolina.

description: Physician, botanist and zoologist (1730-1791)
description_wiki: Alexander Garden FRSE FRS (January 1730 – 15 April 1791) was a Scottish physician, botanist and zoologist.

description: largest city in the U.S. state of South Carolina
description_wiki: Charleston is the most populous city in the U.S. state of South Carolina.

aliases: University of North Carolina Chapel Hill Press
description: American academic publisher
description_wiki: The University of North Carolina Press (UNC Press), founded in 1922, is a nonprofit university press associated with the University of North Carolina.

description: 1775–1783 war between Great Britain and the Thirteen Colonies, which won independence as the United States of America
description_wiki: The American Revolutionary War (April 19, 1775 – September 3, 1783), also known as the Revolutionary War or American War of Independence, was the armed conflict that comprised the final eight years of the broader American Revolution, in which American Patriot forces organized as the Continental Army and commanded by George Washington defeated the British Army.

description: military unit
description_wiki: Lee's Legion (also known as the 2nd Partisan Corps) was a military unit within the Continental Army during the American Revolution.

description: Christian church in England and the Mother Church of the worldwide Anglican Communion
description_wiki: The Church of England (C of E) is the established Christian church in England and the Crown Dependencies.

description: evangelical Christian movement
description_wiki: Baptists are a Protestant tradition of Christianity distinguished by baptizing only believers (believer's baptism) and doing so by total immersion.

description: historic Baptist church in Charleston, South Carolina, United States
description_wiki: First Baptist Church  is a Baptist  church in Charleston, South Carolina.

description: American politician (1746-1825)
description_wiki: Charles Cotesworth Pinckney (February 25, 1746 – August 16, 1825) was an American statesman, military officer and Founding Father who served as United States Minister to France from 1796 to 1797.

description: 1776 assertion of colonial America's independence from Great Britain
description_wiki: The Declaration of Independence, formally The unanimous Declaration of the thirteen united States of America in the original printing, is the founding document of the United States.

aliases: Constitution of the United States of America
description: supreme law of the United States of America since March 4th, 1789
Portuguese wine
description_wiki: Madeira is a fortified wine made on the Portuguese island of Madeira, in the North Atlantic Ocean, just under 400 kilometers (250 mi) north of the Canary Islands, Spain and 520 kilometers (320 mi) west of Morocco.

description_wiki: Harvard University is a private Ivy League research university in Cambridge, Massachusetts, United States.

description_wiki: Angola, officially the Republic of Angola, is a country on the western coast of Southern Africa.

description_wiki: Ireland is an island in the North Atlantic Ocean, in Northwestern Europe.

description_wiki: Edgefield is a town in and the county seat of Edgefield County, South Carolina, United States.

description: 1861–1865 civil war in the United States between the North and the South over the enslavement of African-Americans
description_wiki: The American Civil War (April 12, 1861 – May 26, 1865; also known by other names) was a civil war in the United States between the Union ("the North") and the Confederacy ("the South"), which was formed in 1861 by states that had seceded from the Union to preserve slavery in the United States, which they saw as threatened because of the election of Abraham Lincoln and the growing abolitionist movement in the North.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Kentucky (US:  , UK: ), officially the Commonwealth of Kentucky, is a landlocked state in the Southeastern region of the United States.

description_wiki: Clemson University ([note a]) is a public land-grant research university near Clemson, South Carolina, United States.

description: bank building in Charleston, United States of America
Confederate general (1807-1891)
description_wiki: Joseph Eggleston Johnston (February 3, 1807 – March 21, 1891) was an American military officer and politician who served in the United States Army during the Mexican–American War and in the Seminole Wars.

description: city in North Carolina, United States of America
description_wiki: Greensboro ( ; locally ) is a city in Guilford County, North Carolina, United States, and its county seat.

description: state of the United States of America
description_wiki: Massachusetts (   MASS-ə-CHOO-sits, -⁠zits; Massachusett: Muhsachuweesut [məhswatʃəwiːsət]), officially the Commonwealth of Massachusetts, is a state in the New England region of the Northeastern United States.

description: Canadian politician (1862–1943)
description_wiki: John Fletcher Mitchell (October 12, 1862 – October 6, 1943) was a politician in Manitoba, Canada.

description: county seat of Greenville County, South Carolina, United States
description_wiki: Greenville ( GREEN-vil; locally  GREEN-vəl) is a city in Greenville County, South Carolina, United States, and its county seat.

description: largest city in Pennsylvania, United States
description_wiki: Philadelphia (  FIL-ə-DEL-fee-ə), colloquially referred to as Philly, is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Pennsylvania, and the sixth-most populous city in the United States, with a Census-estimated population of 1,574,281 in July 2025.

aliases: New England Region of the United States
description: region of the northeasternmost United States
description_wiki: New England is a region consisting of six states in the Northeastern United States: Connecticut, Maine, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, Rhode Island, and Vermont.
